description_long: This is a whole watermelon with a nearly spherical shape and a thick, glossy rind. The surface is predominantly dark green, adorned with irregular, mottled patches and stripes of lighter yellow-green. The rind displays a natural sheen, with a few small blemishes and a visible stem scar at one end. The patterning is organic and non-uniform, with the lighter markings radiating outward from the stem area. The overall appearance is fresh and typical of a mature watermelon, with no visible cuts or openings. The object is solid and dense, suggesting a juicy, red interior filled with seeds, though none are visible from the outside.
description: A round, glossy watermelon with dark green skin and irregular yellow-green mottled stripes, featuring a visible stem scar and natural blemishes.
description_view_0: This view shows the watermelon’s side, highlighting the irregular yellow-green mottled patches on a dark green background.
description_view_1: This view focuses on the stem end, where lighter green stripes radiate outward from the stem scar, creating a starburst effect.
description_view_2: This side view displays more of the mottled pattern, with a small blemish visible on the rind.
description_view_3: This view also centers on the stem end, showing the radiating lighter stripes and the stem remnant more clearly.
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottled and striped pattern with lighter yellow-green patches and stripes on a dark green background, radiating from the stem area.
attribute_rind_markings: Small brown blemish, stem scar, and scattered lighter patches and stripes; markings are organic and unevenly distributed.
